label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me think about this question about causation. So, the scenario is: - There's a box of colored pencils. - Only bears are allowed to take pencils; hedgehogs are not. - The hedgehog wasn't present when the rule was announced, so it doesn't know the rule. - Both the bear and the hedgehog took pencils out of the box six times alternately. - Then, they both took a pencil at the same time. - Later, a polar bear (another type of bear) approached the box to get a pencil for homework, but there were no pencils left. - The polar bear couldn't finish homework. The question is: Did the hedgehog cause the problem? Options are: Yes / No Let me analyze the causation here: 1. The rule: Only bears can take pencils, hedgehogs cannot. But the hedgehog wasn't told this rule. 2. The bear and hedgehog alternately took pencils 6 times. So, they each took 6 pencils. 3. Then, they both took
